heading: Staff corps of the Navy
label: 8090 Staff corps of the Navy
notes: Amendments2024—Subsec. (a)(5) to (9). , added pars. (5) to (8) and redesignated former par. (5) as (9).Pub. L. 118–159, § 923(a)Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “staff corps specified in subsection (a)” for “Medical Corps, the Dental Corps, the Judge Advocate General’s Corps, and the Chaplain Corps”.Pub. L. 118–159, § 923(b)2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 5150 of this title2016—Subsec. (b)(2). , substituted “The Secretary” for “Subject to subsection (c), the Secretary”.Pub. L. 114–328, § 502(hh)(1)Subsec. (c). , struck out subsec. (c) which read as follows: “The Secretary of the Navy, whenever the needs of the service require, may convene a selection board under to select an officer in the Nurse Corps or in the Medical Service Corps (if such corps has been established under subsection (a)) for promotion to the grade of rear admiral, in the case of an officer in the Nurse Corps, or rear admiral (lower half), in the case of an officer in the Medical Service Corps. An officer promoted pursuant to such a selection shall be appointed by the Secretary to the position of Director of the Nurse Corps or Director of the Medical Service Corps, respectively, for a term of four years, to serve at the pleasure of the Secretary.”Pub. L. 114–328, § 502(hh)(2)section 611(a) of this title2013—Subsec. (c). struck out at end “For the purpose of computing the total number of flag officers in the staff corps of the Navy under , an officer so appointed shall be considered an additional number in grade.”Pub. L. 112–239section 526 of this title2002—Subsec. (c). substituted “for promotion to the grade of rear admiral, in the case of an officer in the Nurse Corps, or rear admiral (lower half), in the case of an officer in the Medical Service Corps” for “for promotion to the grade of rear admiral (lower half)” in first sentence.Pub. L. 107–3141991—Subsec. (c). substituted “section 526” for “section 5444”.Pub. L. 102–1901987—Subsec. (c). made technical amendment to directory language of . See 1986 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 100–26Pub. L. 99–661, § 1343(a)(23)1986— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 99–433section 5155 of this titleSubsec. (c). , as amended by , substituted “rear admiral (lower half)” for “commodore”.Pub. L. 99–661Pub. L. 100–261981—Subsec. (c). substituted “commodore” for “commodore admiral”.Pub. L. 97–86substituted “Nurse Corps or in the Medical Service Corps (if such corps has been established under subsection (a)) for promotion to the grade of commodore admiral” for “Nurse Corps or Medical Service Corps for promotion to the grade of commodore admiral or rear admiral, as appropriate”, substituted “An officer promoted pursuant to such a selection shall be appointed by the Secretary to the position of Director of the Nurse Corps or Director of the Medical Service Corps, respectively, for a term of four years, to serve at the pleasure of the Secretary” for “An officer so selected shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, for a term of four years to serve in the position, respectively, of Director of the Nurse Corps or Director of the Medical Service Corps”, and inserted provision that for the purpose of computing the total number of flag officers in the staff corps of the Navy under , an officer so appointed shall be considered an additional number in grade.Pub. L. 97–22section 5444 of this title

children.text: such other staff corps as may be established by the Secretary of the Navy under subsection (b).

The Secretary of the Navy may establish staff corps of the Navy in addition to the staff corps specified in subsection (a). The Secretary may designate commissioned officers in, and may assign members to, any such staff corps.
The Secretary of the Navy may provide for the appointment of the chief of any staff corps established under this subsection.
